diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Itch is the hallmark distinguishing allergic conjunctivitis from infective causes; 'no itch, think again about allergy'.
Bacterial conjunctivitis is typically gritty/irritable rather than intensely itchy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear watery (serous) discharge, no purulence or lash matting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Watery discharge fits allergic (and viral) conjunctivitis.
Absence of thick purulent discharge and lash matting argues strongly against bacterial infection.
Simultaneous nasal allergic symptoms indicate systemic aeroallergen exposure (allergic rhinoconjunctivitis).
Bilateral symmetric lid oedema from histamine-driven vascular permeability fits ocular allergy; video-observable, patient-reportable.
Periorbital swelling mandates exclusion of orbital cellulitis; bilateral symmetry and painlessness argue strongly against it.
Sudden facial swelling mandates an airway/systemic screen before benign closure.
Redness is common to all conjunctivitis; non-specific on its own but consistent with the allergic picture.
Marked chemosis reflects acute histamine-mediated vascular permeability; severe cases may be partly video-observable but graded on exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tarsal papillary reaction on eyelid eversion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Papillae (vs follicles) on the tarsal conjunctiva favour an allergic/immune reaction; requires clinician eyelid eversion, so sealed and not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visual change, no deep eye pain, no photophobia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pain, photophobia and visual change makes a sight-threatening red eye very unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lip/tongue/throat swelling, no dyspnoea, no urticaria, no faintness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative airway/systemic screen excludes anaphylaxis and confirms the reaction is localised to the ocular/nasal surface.
Absence of fever and systemic upset argues against orbital cellulitis and systemic infection; clinician-measured vitals are sealed, but the patient can report feeling non-feverish.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Bilateral itch-predominant watery red eyes with concurrent rhinitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these are near-diagnostic of allergic rhinoconjunctivitis and separate it from infective and sight-threatening causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / swabs for uncomplicated allergic conjunctivitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Allergic conjunctivitis is a clinical diagnosis; no acute laboratory or microbiology testing is warranted, and the AI should not over-order.
Appropriate in person if diagnosis is uncertain or red flags emerge (to exclude corneal involvement/keratitis); not obtainable via telehealth, hence a reason to safety-net rather than a live request.
Outpatient work-up for recurrent allergic rhinoconjunctivitis to identify triggers; not part of acute management.
